channel.title: Comments for page "Katz and Dogs"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Katz and Dogs"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just wanted to let you know I am rooting for you! Glad I could help with this! (0ᴗ0✿)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Replies to nitpicks
– hide block

Is "very tiring" really what you tell a superior at work asking about your day?

Changed it to "very good"

Man, this Katz fellow sounds like the guy who keeps getting "not a team player" on his annual review and can't figure out why.

Messed with that line a bit, hopefully made the intent more clear

50MB is 50,000,000 characters. If we are charitable and assume words inside the legal document are six characters long on average, that's over eight million words, which for context is about twenty times the length of the Lord of the Rings trilogy, or about four times longer than A Song of Ice and Fire, which is what Game of Thrones is based on. That is a shitload of text, and memorizing it in less than a year's time is totally impossible. Reading it in under a week would be unbelievable, IMO.

I changed it to a docx file. With diagrams and all, 50MB would be reasonable

Fixed the dialog thing you mentioned

This strikes me as weird: why does a group capable of transdimensionally kidnapping people care about earth money?

Never said it was Earth money they were after.

I also tinkered a bit with the dialog in the opening part, since you pointed that out. I hope that most of the issues you had with this are fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Conceptually, this is fun. I have a few complaints which center around dialogue and statistics.

a bunch of nitpicking
– hide block

The intro conversation is pretty stilted and I don't know anyone who talks like either Katz or Graham. Stuff like this:

maninthecomputer: yes, how's your day?

skatz: it's very tiring so far, if you want to know, though I doubt that's why you flagged me down

maninthecomputer: sharp, as always.

Is "very tiring" really what you tell a superior at work asking about your day?

skatz: and i'm not really a field agent, can't you get someone with experience?

Man, this Katz fellow sounds like the guy who keeps getting "not a team player" on his annual review and can't figure out why.

This part made me wince:

  • maninthecomputer has uploaded file goi-344-known-policies-and-laws.txt

WARNING: DOCUMENT EXCEEDS 50MB IN SIZE

50MB is 50,000,000 characters. If we are charitable and assume words inside the legal document are six characters long on average, that's over eight million words, which for context is about twenty times the length of the Lord of the Rings trilogy, or about four times longer than A Song of Ice and Fire, which is what Game of Thrones is based on. That is a shitload of text, and memorizing it in less than a year's time is totally impossible. Reading it in under a week would be unbelievable, IMO.

"Your trial is tomorrow, Shock," the guard said in his usual deep voice that reminded Shock of someone banging a rock against an iron plate. "You'll want to prepare for the trial."

I think the second quoted section could just be "You'll want to prepare."

the true motive of GoI-344 is to capture and imprison civilians under the pretense of its "laws," then ransom them at high prices using "bail."

This strikes me as weird: why does a group capable of transdimensionally kidnapping people care about earth money?

For now it's a novote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So if the FSF Delivery is here, is this Katz from Straight On Til Morning?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great palpitating amazeballs. Maybe this just hit me at the right moment, but this is [one specific iteration of] the sort of thing that keeps me coming back to the Foundationverse. Well done.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
As mentioned, this is a tale for the OCT contest. It's just dawning on me now how bad of an idea it was to enter a character that actively avoided the Foundation in a contest mostly involving characters from the Foundation.

Thanks to Lazar Lyusternik, GrimmCreeper, OthellotheCat, AnnRKissPhD and spikebrennan for critique! I stole formatting from a couple of djkaktus skips as well. A lot of this was inspired by Termination_Order by DrClef.

Sheldon Katz was created by spikebrennan, and Shock was created by me.
